item.title: 【used】Squier / FSR Vintage Modified 77 Jazz Bass TBL 3.760kg #ICS13072792【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $891
item.description:
$891 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Squier, a brand under the Fender umbrella, offers a wide range of models with excellent cost performance.

This model is a Vintage Modified '77 Jazz Bass, produced as a limited FSR (Factory Special Run).

The body is made of basswood, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

With its 1970s style featuring block inlays and binding, and a special color exclusive to the FSR, this is a truly unique instrument.

It is equipped with two Duncan Designed JB101 Single Coil pickups.

Based on the clear and articulate sound characteristic of a Jazz Bass, it combines power and ease of playability.

The Vintage Modified series is a particularly highly regarded and popular series within Squier.

It boasts a good balance of vintage-like looks and sound, and excellent playability, offering a level of quality that exceeds its price.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition. The body and paintwork show minor scratches and small dents. Metal parts show some tarnishing.
The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.
Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone
Nut width: 38.0mm
Bridge string spacing: 19.0mm
Scale: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: Unknown
Country of manufacture: INDONESIA

During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: None

Text: Mizune Ishikawa

Inspection Staff: Oshima

Product Photos: Tei

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

◯GIB and TONIQ Warranty Information

●Permanent Free Maintenance

Instruments purchased from our store are eligible for permanent free "String Vibration Setup" (normally ¥4,400). (Reservation required)

・Please present your warranty card with your name filled in.

・Only applicable to the person who purchased the instrument at a GIB or TONIQ physical store or online shop.

・Please bear the cost of shipping and other miscellaneous expenses.

●Re-purchase Price Guarantee

If you sell your purchased item within 6 months of purchase, we guarantee the buyback price. The price is 50% for new items and 70% for used items.

●Defect Warranty

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we will repair (or refund if repair is not possible) any malfunctions that occur during normal use within a certain warranty period from the date of purchase free of charge.

The warranty period is 3 months for used products and 1 year for new products.

Please note that the following cases will incur a charge even within the warranty period:

* Malfunctions and damage caused by transportation, movement, dropping, etc.

* Replacement of consumables.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by fire, earthquake, salt damage, gas damage, or abnormal voltage.

* Malfunctions and damage caused by handling contrary to the precautions described in the instruction manual.

* Malfunctions or damage to parts repaired or modified by a party other than our company.

* If the warranty certificate does not have your name, model name, serial number, etc. filled in, or if the wording has been altered.

* If the warranty certificate is not presented or is lost.

(Warranty is only provided for purchases made in Japan.)

● Up to 15% OFF with Trade-in

When you use our trade-in service at the time of purchase, the sales price will be 10% off. (Consignment items are excluded. This offer is only applicable if the trade-in value is 15% or more of the selling price.)

Furthermore, if you trade in an instrument eligible for our monthly buyback campaign, the discount rate will be 15%.

We will ship with sufficient packaging.

We also ship internationally.

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.

item.title: Fender PB62-80 Precision Bass - JV Series — $1,967.33
item.description:
$1,967.33 · Very Good · Found Sound

Fender PB62-80 Precision Bass with Olympic white finish, Made in Japan, with a JV serial number. The JV era of Fender marks the period where USA production standards were declining, so they singed a deal to produce Fender branded instruments made by luthiers in Japan whose production standards were nothing but excellence.

Alder body and maple neck with a rosewood fretboard, 34" scale, 7.25" radius, vintage C shape. Original Fender USA PB-Vintage split single coil pickups. Pots have been replaced (original included), ashtray set and thumb rest installed by previous owner.

This bass reflects the care and consideration that was put into it, with decently low action and flat wound strings, it plays remarkably well. Neck date 9 Oct 1984, Fujigen Gakki factory.

Includes a Fender hardshell case. Serviced before sale, setup with flatwound strings and ashray covers, thumb rest added. Original pots included but upgraded pots installed. Original pickups.

Found Sound Australia Pty Ltd.
110 Albert St
Brunswick East, VIC 3057
Australia

item.title: Jackson X Series Spectre Bass SBX IV IN Metallic Red - Metallic Finish - Excellent w/ Soft Case — $422.99
item.description:
$422.99 · Excellent · Audio Trader

Condition Details:
Please check all photos of the item carefully, as they form part of the description of the condition of the item. This Jackson X Series Spectre Bass SBX IV IN Metallic Red is in excellent condition, showing minimal signs of use with no significant issues. The metallic red finish is virtually flawless, and the hardware gleams as if new. This bass has been well-maintained and is ready to deliver outstanding performance.

What's Included:
This listing comes with a Soft Case, providing essential protection for your bass during transport and storage. The case is plush-lined, ensuring your instrument remains scratch-free and secure.

About the Item:
The Jackson X Series Spectre Bass SBX IV IN Metallic Red is a high-performance instrument designed for bass players who demand exceptional tone and playability. Known for its stunning metallic red finish and sleek body design, this bass guitar stands out on stage and offers a comfortable playing experience. Its reputation for powerful sound and reliable construction makes it a sought-after choice for both aspiring and professional musicians.

Key Specifications/Features:
Crafted with a basswood body, the Spectre Bass SBX IV delivers a balanced tone with enhanced midrange punch. The maple neck features a fast-playing profile, topped with a rosewood fingerboard for smooth action and effortless playability. Equipped with active electronics, this bass provides a versatile sound palette with powerful low-end and crisp highs. The high-output J-style pickups ensure clarity and definition in every note, making it perfect for any genre. An adjustable bridge allows for precise intonation adjustments to suit your playing style.

This Jackson X Series Spectre Bass SBX IV IN Metallic Red is an excellent choice for any bassist looking to elevate their performance with a striking and reliable instrument.

item.title: 【used】YAMAHA / TRBX605FM 2025 4.03kg #ILN123090【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $919
item.description:
$919 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Yamaha is a leading Japanese brand that has supported the music scene both domestically and internationally since the early days of guitar and bass manufacturing in Japan. It is truly a brand that Japan can be proud of.

Top musicians who transcend time, borders, and genres, including Nathan East, the next-generation technician Vincen García (who has recently gained explosive popularity worldwide), and Seiji Kameda in Japan, all use Yamaha basses (titles omitted).

The reason why so many renowned artists choose Yamaha instruments is undoubtedly due to the overwhelming quality of Japanese craftsmanship that the Yamaha brand has built up over many years, and the unwavering reliability it provides in the field.

While Yamaha boasts many famous models such as the SBV, SG, and BB, the TRB series is equally beloved.

The body top features beautifully grained Flame Maple, and the back is made of well-balanced Alder.

It boasts an elegant look.

The neck features a robust 5-piece construction combining maple and mahogany.

This technique of laminating multiple pieces of wood together minimizes left-right and individual variations, resulting in greater stability.

The fingerboard is rosewood.

The pickups are original dual-coil pickups using Alnico V magnets.

They deliver a low-noise, clear tone, from deep bass to high frequencies in the upper register, ensuring a clear sound image even when playing chords.

The original 3-band EQ is highly effective, allowing for significant tone control with intuitive operation.

From punchy slap sounds to singing solo tones with emphasized mids, it instantly enables versatile sound shaping to suit various ensembles.

The modern and refined body design combines smooth playability with excellent instrument balance.

Furthermore, this instrument features a white pearl pickguard fitted as a slap step.

By filling the gap between the strings and the body, it prevents fingers from getting too far under the strings, improving playability. While being low-noise, it possesses an overwhelming power that firmly supports the foundation of the ensemble.

With minimal muddiness in the bass, the clear, well-defined, and fast-paced Hi-Fi tone faithfully follows everything from beautiful piano-like chord playing to high-speed slap bass.

This guitar has the potential to excel in any genre, from pop accompaniment to intense rock and technically demanding fusion.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have a pickguard attached. There are no noticeable scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Active/Passive Switch, Bass, Middle, Treble
Nut Width: 42.9mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2025
Country of Manufacture: INDONESIA

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

item.title: Columbus P-Bass 1970s - Electric Bass - Natural - Good w/ Soft Case — $439.74
item.description:
$439.74 · Excellent · Audio Trader

Columbus P-Bass 1970s - Electric Bass - Natural Condition Details:
Columbus P-Bass 1970s - Electric Bass - Natural
This Columbus P-Bass 1970s - Electric Bass - Natural is in good condition and has been thoroughly tested to ensure all eight functions operate correctly. The instrument shows only minor signs of use throughout its surface, structure, and overall presentation, maintaining solid integrity that reflects careful handling over the years. You can expect reliable performance with the authentic character that comes from a well-maintained vintage instrument.

What's Included:
This listing includes a protective soft case that will keep your bass secure during transport and storage. Having proper protection is essential for maintaining the condition of any vintage instrument, making this a complete package ready for immediate use.

About the Item:
Columbus guitars from the 1970s represent an interesting chapter in electric instrument manufacturing, offering players access to classic P-Bass styling and functionality from this distinctive era. These instruments appeal to bassists who appreciate vintage aesthetics and the unique tonal characteristics that come from 1970s construction methods and materials. The natural finish showcases the wood grain beautifully while providing that unmistakable vintage appeal that many musicians seek.

This represents an excellent opportunity to own a piece of 1970s bass history in good playing condition at an accessible price point.

item.title: 【used】Seymour Duncan / DJ-110M ’75 2003 4.15kg #51340【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,267
item.description:
$1,267 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Seymour Duncan is extremely famous as a pickup brand, but their bass guitars are also quite popular.

This is the DJ-110M '75.

This is a Jazz Bass from the Traditional Series, meticulously assembled in a domestic workshop (ESP), bearing the name of Seymour Duncan, a world-renowned pickup manufacturer.

This instrument faithfully respects the specifications of a 1975 Jazz Bass.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple. The neck binding and block inlays give it a cool, refined look characteristic of the 70s.

Naturally, it comes standard with genuine Seymour Duncan SJB pickups.

The tight, focused bass and punchy, clear treble are purely and powerfully output by the Duncan pickups.

The sound is an open and clear tone, truly capturing the essence of a 70s Jazz Bass.

The powerful, punchy attack when fingerpicking, as well as the crisp, responsive feel when slapping, are major attractions.

It also boasts excellent rigidity and playability thanks to reliable Japanese woodworking techniques, allowing you to directly experience the rich, full tone inherent in passive basses.

Don't miss this opportunity!

Fret life is approximately 90%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have two dents on the side of the body. There are also scattered small dents and scratches throughout.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were found.

A used Jazz Bass with a vintage feel has arrived!

This is the most versatile and popular 1962 specification model, featuring an alder body and rosewood fingerboard. It boasts a well-balanced range and responsive feel, making it a ready-to-use model for any genre or style.

As this item dates from around 1993-1994, there are dents, scratches, and lacquer paint splatters.
The nut has likely been replaced, the frets refretted, and the frets have been leveled, so the playability is excellent. The control panel has also likely been replaced, but the electronics appear to be original.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

Weight ≈ 3.99 kg
Accessories: General-purpose soft case

The Made in Japan Heritage series pursues the ultimate playing experience that vintage instruments offer to players. Supervised by Mark Kendrick, a renowned master builder at the Fender Custom Shop with a deep understanding of vintage instruments, it incorporates specifications that will captivate vintage enthusiasts. All models in the Heritage series are designed based on profiling data of USA products, with body and neck shapes adopted to match the era. From the cross-wire pickups and pickup settings and tone to the reproduction of body colors, every detail inherits the authentic lineage of Fender.

The Made in Japan Heritage 60s Jazz Bass features an alder body with a lacquer finish that deepens in color with age. Every specification of this instrument—from the 7.25-inch radius 60s C-shaped maple neck with 20 vintage-style frets, to the vintage-style bridge with four threaded steel saddles and the specially selected pickups for the Heritage series—is meticulously crafted to deliver classic playability and sweet vintage tone.

It is in relatively good condition, although there are some minor scratches and light dents. The control section has been replaced from a 3-knob setup to a 2-knob setup with 2 CTS potentiometers.
The truss rod has plenty of adjustment range, and the neck condition is good with no major issues.

Weight ≈ 4.22 kg
Accessories: General-purpose soft case

item.title: 【used】Fender / Player Jazz Bass BLK 2021 4.08kg #MX21055153【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,079
item.description:
$1,079 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorius, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee (titles omitted).

The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.

Its sharp, clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to receive overwhelming support and shines brightly even today.

This model is a cost-effective Jazz Bass from the Player series, made in Mexico.

The body is made of alder.

The fingerboard is made of Pau Ferro.

It has a warmth similar to rosewood, but provides a clearer, more responsive attack.

Furthermore, as this instrument was manufactured in 2021, the back of the headstock features a "75th Anniversary" logo celebrating Fender's 75th anniversary.

The pickups are Player Series Alnico 5 Single-Coil pickups, specially designed for this series. It delivers a punchy attack and a genuine Fender tone that leads the ensemble.

Perhaps influenced by its Mexican manufacturing environment, the sound has a distinctly different character from Japanese-made models, with a somewhat open and dynamic feel.

While based on the classic Jazz Bass sound, it incorporates modern updates such as a flatter fingerboard radius, making it extremely playable.

Don't miss this opportunity!

Fret life is approximately 90%.

Truss rod is in good condition.

Neck is in good condition.

There is a dent on the headstock of the body and paintwork.

Electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass, RARE, ’75-US Bound, Old Candy, 2004, USA Pickups, Hard Case — $1,450
item.description:
$1,450 · Good · String Switch Noise


2004-05, Crafted In Japan, “R” serial


This is one of the highest end Fender Japan Jazz Bass models, JB75-90US MH


A non catalogue release, and near impossible to find, especially outside of Japan


It is a modelled after a ’75 Jazz Bass, with all the standout features of this time


Including a bound neck, pearloid block inlays, and matching colour headstock


Rarer still, it is finished in an amazing Old Candy Apple Red


Three ply white pickguard


Complete with finger “tug bar”


Factory Fender USA, Alnico vintage, single coil pickups


Excellent balance between them, with a nice variation in tone individually


Great clarity, warm vintage tones, whilst keeping the classic J bass snarl and growl


As a bonus, it has had a full wiring upgrade to USA spec

-CTS (USA) 250k pots

-Switchcraft (USA) jack

-Vintage style cloth wiring

-Paper in oil 0.01mfd tone cap


7.25” board, vintage frets, “C” profile


Standard 34” full scale


Slim 39mm nut width neck


Maple one piece neck


Rosewood fretboard


Neck binding with pearloid block inlays


1970’s era CBS headstock logo


Bullet truss rod, “Skunk stripe” neck


3 bolt, tilt o matic neck plate


“F” bridge cover in chrome


Excellent overall condition, a rarity not to miss out on


Includes a Hard Case




ITEM CONDITION

All machinery, electrical, and pickups are without issue. Some very light surface marks, some very small impressions/dings/dimples, some small paint nicks and chips, very light aging and tarnish to hardware - as pictured.





POSTAGE & SHIPPING NOTE:

- Upon cleared payment, all small items (pedals, wiring etc) are usually posted within 24-72hr


- Guitars and Basses are packed on Monday (courier collection Tuesday) and Thursday (for courier collection Friday)


Meaning, if your purchase is on a Thursday (after 1pm), Friday, Saturday, Sunday, or Monday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Tuesday.

If your purchase is on Monday (after 1pm), Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Friday.


We take great care and pride in making sure guitars are carefully set up and packed correctly for transport, so we do not rush this step.




The String Switch Noise store operates primarily as an online business.

However, we do have a dedicated showroom 30min south of the Brisbane CBD.

By appointment, we are available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm (and Saturday and Sunday by availability) to test out and pick up purchased items.

Please call or contact us directly through the website.

String Switch Noise



---Shipping---

--- International delivery is possible for most items and can be arranged at exact cost of shipping. However, there are some restrictions with regions and package size. Please contact us first BEFORE making any purchase to check if an item can be delivered, and to organise the ACTUAL delivery costs. International shipping pricing (if found on listing) is an estimate only (it may be more, but it also may be less). Any applicable customs, duties, or taxes that your country may charge is solely the responsibility of you the buyer. Any questions you may have about this should be directed to your local countries authorities.

--- We proudly strive to send out all orders within 24 hours of purchase and/or receiving cleared payment (apart from holidays and weekends). However please allow up to 72hrs for packing and courier pickups. Once your item is packed you will receive notification and relevant tracking number and courier/postal details.

--- Small items such as effect pedals and wiring harnesses are sent via Australia Post standard service, with tracking ONLY. Both domestically and internationally. No responsibility will be taken from us for the loss or damage of an item by Australia Post. At your request, signature upon delivery or postal insurance can be added for the additional cost. Please contact us before purchase, or immediately after purchase.

--- Australia wide, guitars are sent via courier delivery with full insurance, tracking, and signature upon delivery services. Guitar packages MAY be sent via Australia Post if the delivery area is in a remote region/outside normal courier delivery areas/outside normal costs (and the package size is under 105cm long).

--- Courier deliveries can NOT be sent to a PO Box, Parcel Locker, or Parcel Collect service. They must be sent to a physical address, where someone can sign for the item on arrival.

--- All items are packed to the highest degree for transit. Proudly, our packaging is at the highest of standards, with the best reputation you will find anywhere in the industry. This comes from 3500+ guitars (and counting) being sent without issue both Australia wide and internationally over 10+ years (and counting). A great amount of pride and detail goes into making sure every item arrives safe and in a timely manner. Pedals and small items are packed in multiple layering of bubble wrap and double corrugated shipping cardboard. Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

item.description:

2004-05, Crafted In Japan, “R” serial


Extremely sought after, and RARE to find, JB65B-US MH model


This is one of Fender Japan’s higher end models


A non catalogue release, and near impossible to find, especially outside of Japan


It is a modelled after a ’65 Jazz Bass, with all the standout features of this time


Including a bound neck, dot inlays, and matching colour headstock


Factory fitted with Fender USA Vintage Alnico Pickups


Classic JB sound and tone, lots of attack and growl


Rarer still, it is finished in the always stunning Candy Apple Red

(I have only ever seen examples of sunburst and white for this JB65MH model)


Three ply white pickguard


Maple one piece neck


Slab Rosewood fretboard


“C” profile, 39mm nut neck width


7.25” board, vintage frets, standard 34” scale


1965 era features…

-Period correct spaghetti headstock logo

-Matching colour headstock

-Bound neck with dot inlays

-Copper shield plates in cavities

-Rear headstock strap pin

-Spiral saddle bridge

-Large base plate open gear tuners


Excellent overall condition, a rarity not to miss out on


Includes a Hard Case




ITEM CONDITION

All machinery, electrical, and pickups are without issue. Some very light surface marks, some very small impressions/dings/dimples, NO major paint chips, very light aging to chrome, some micro movement between the plastic binding/maple/rosewood (NOT cracking or splitting, just normal shrinkage between the 3 different materials, like most bound necks) - as pictured.




POSTAGE & SHIPPING NOTE:

- Upon cleared payment, all small items (pedals, wiring etc) are usually posted within 24-72hr


- Guitars and Basses are packed on Monday (courier collection Tuesday) and Thursday (for courier collection Friday)


Meaning, if your purchase is on a Thursday (after 1pm), Friday, Saturday, Sunday, or Monday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Tuesday.

If your purchase is on Monday (after 1pm), Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Friday.


We take great care and pride in making sure guitars are carefully set up and packed correctly for transport, so we do not rush this step.




The String Switch Noise store operates primarily as an online business.

However, we do have a dedicated showroom 30min south of the Brisbane CBD.

By appointment, we are available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm (and Saturday and Sunday by availability) to test out and pick up purchased items.

Please call or contact us directly through the website.

String Switch Noise



---Shipping---

--- International delivery is possible for most items and can be arranged at exact cost of shipping. However, there are some restrictions with regions and package size. Please contact us first BEFORE making any purchase to check if an item can be delivered, and to organise the ACTUAL delivery costs. International shipping pricing (if found on listing) is an estimate only (it may be more, but it also may be less). Any applicable customs, duties, or taxes that your country may charge is solely the responsibility of you the buyer. Any questions you may have about this should be directed to your local countries authorities.

--- We proudly strive to send out all orders within 24 hours of purchase and/or receiving cleared payment (apart from holidays and weekends). However please allow up to 72hrs for packing and courier pickups. Once your item is packed you will receive notification and relevant tracking number and courier/postal details.

--- Small items such as effect pedals and wiring harnesses are sent via Australia Post standard service, with tracking ONLY. Both domestically and internationally. No responsibility will be taken from us for the loss or damage of an item by Australia Post. At your request, signature upon delivery or postal insurance can be added for the additional cost. Please contact us before purchase, or immediately after purchase.

--- Australia wide, guitars are sent via courier delivery with full insurance, tracking, and signature upon delivery services. Guitar packages MAY be sent via Australia Post if the delivery area is in a remote region/outside normal courier delivery areas/outside normal costs (and the package size is under 105cm long).

--- Courier deliveries can NOT be sent to a PO Box, Parcel Locker, or Parcel Collect service. They must be sent to a physical address, where someone can sign for the item on arrival.

--- All items are packed to the highest degree for transit. Proudly, our packaging is at the highest of standards, with the best reputation you will find anywhere in the industry. This comes from 3500+ guitars (and counting) being sent without issue both Australia wide and internationally over 10+ years (and counting). A great amount of pride and detail goes into making sure every item arrives safe and in a timely manner. Pedals and small items are packed in multiple layering of bubble wrap and double corrugated shipping cardboard. Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

item.title: 【new】Capurso Guitars / Spiral Deluxe 5st Burl Top Sea Green Burst 3.795kg 【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $5,019
item.description:
$5,019 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Special Price ¥649,000 → ¥550,000

This item has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Capurso Guitars is a high-end brand based in Italy that manufactures high-quality, handmade basses.

It is known to be trusted and used by many musicians, including Gabriele Costa and Giacomo Turra. (Titles omitted)

The instruments crafted by builder Andrea Capurso are consistently traditional, experimental, and musical.

He translates brilliant concepts into instruments with solid skill.

The Spiral is a work that takes Fender's traditional style as its base while incorporating more modern development.

This is a 24-fret, active, modern-style bass modeled after a JB type.

The body top features a striking Poplar Burl finish, giving it a very beautiful look.

The back is made of light ash and the fretboard is maple, a classic combination.

The tuning machines and bridge are HIPSHOT.

A global standard.

The pickups are Italian MAMA Pickups.

The acoustic design already ensures excellent balance of string vibration, and the pickups simply output a clear sound.

A truly beautiful design.

The mini-switch is a tap switch for the bridge pickup.

Tapping activates only the neck-side coil.

This makes the sound sharper and clearer than the powerful dual-coil setting.

Those who prefer a JB-like texture will likely prefer the tapped state.

The preamp is a 3-band EQ from Italian manufacturer Xander Electronics.

The frequencies are 30Hz for bass, 250Hz for middle, and 10kHz for treble.

The bass includes sub-band frequencies, and the treble includes presence components; boosting them results in a high-range and speedy sound.

The middle is slightly lower at 250Hz.

Rather than using it for cutting, actively pushing the tone results in a wide range while maintaining a bass-like balance.

In fact, Andrea Capurso, the builder of Capurso Guitars, was heavily involved in the preamp's design.

His philosophy is embodied in the design used in his own work, so the compatibility is naturally excellent.

The playability is extremely light, and the nuance of responding sensitively to even the slightest touch while resonating fully is very impressive.

While multi-string, 24-fret basses often tend to sound too tight, this particular instrument has an open and expansive sound.

The neck quality is extremely high, and to allow you to fully appreciate it, the string action is kept low.

This results in an ultra-smooth playability and a speedy sound.

A masterpiece that elevates the active JB-type style.

Please try it out.

●SPECS
Body: LIGHT ASH
Top: POPLAR BURL
Neck: RED MAPLE
Fretboard: HARD ROLK MAPLE
Scale: 34""""""""
Radius: 16"
Nut: BONE 45 mm
Truss rod: DOUBLE - CARBON FIBER
Neck profile: O GIVAL 20mm - 22 mm
Frets: 6230
Dot: ABALONE
Pickups: MAMA
Preamp: XANDER 3B
Knobs: ALUMINIUM
Bridge: HIPSHOT STYLEB
Tuners: HIPSHOT ULTRALIGHT
Color: SEA GREEN BURST
Finishing: SATIN

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: None

Text: Ryoma Terao

Inspector: Miura

Product Photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Fender Japan JB62-58 3-Tone Sunburst [CIJ O065946] (07/15) — $792.80
item.description:
$792.80 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The Fender Japan 4-string Jazz Bass gJB62-58h has arrived!
Judging by the gCrafted in Japanh label and the gOh serial number, this is believed to be a model manufactured between 1997 and 2000 during the period when they were assembled by Dyna Gakki.
A Jazz Bass reissue model that recreates the 1962 specifications.
It features a basswood body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard.
Its slim, comfortable neck shape and reliable Japanese craftsmanship deliver exceptional playability.
A truly appealing instrument with vintage-style looks and sound!
It shows signs of wear appropriate for its age, such as scratches, dents, and peeling or cracked finish.
Signs of aging, such as rust and tarnish, are visible on the metal parts.
There is paint peeling at the neck joint and the body end, exposing the wood beneath.
Since the pickups were set too low, wefve added sponge shims underneath them to adjust the height.
There are no noticeable issues with the neck, and it is in good playing condition.

Condition Grade: B+: Some scratches are present, but the instrument is suitable for normal use.

item.title: WARWICK 1993 Thumb Bass 4 [A 003649 93] (07/15) — $4,576.32
item.description:
$4,576.32 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Wefve received an excellent example of a 1993 Warwick Thumb Bass gMade in Germanyh that shows surprisingly little wear.

This is the Thumb Bass, an original model by founder Hans-Peter Wilfer that has become an icon of the German company Warwick. Unlike the Streamer series?which was inspired by the Spector NS-2 (and shares a nearly identical shape)?this model features a compact body, a 26-fret fingerboard, and the use of bubinga wood, which defines its unique sound. These distinctive design elements continue to be favored by players around the world to this day.The light, percussive sound derived from the bubinga wood offers a tone unlike any other bass, and combined with the unlimited versatility provided by the MEC 3-band active tone control, it is sure to stimulate the playerfs creativity.

There are virtually no scratches overall; the only visible mark is a slight scuff on the 4th string side of the front pickup. It remains in an incredibly well-preserved condition with minimal signs of use.

Weight: 4.4 kg
Comes with original gig bag and strap lock

Color: Natural
Body Finish: Oiled
Body Wood: Bubinga
Neck Finish: Oiled
Neck Wood: Bubinga
Fretboard Wenge
Frets 24
Scale Length 34
Electronics Active
Pickups MEC JJ

item.title: 【new】Alusonic / Django Standard 5 Special Edition Black 3.58kg #0325509【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,081
item.description:
$4,081 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Special Price ¥594,000 → ¥440,000

Alusonic is an Italian brand that primarily manufactures guitars and basses incorporating aluminum as a material.

Because many elements are designed and manufactured in-house, their products are all pure, highly consistent, and possess a perfect balance at a high level.

The Django is the company's flagship model, featuring a refined body shape and unique electronics.

Its extremely light weight is a major attraction, significantly impacting both sound and playability.

The body is made of Sassafras.

Similar to Ash, but characterized by its lighter weight.

It's also famous for being the first wood Fender considered using, and several guitar brands have adopted it in recent years.

The finish is a thin acrylic that allows the wood grain to show through.

While its extremely light weight might raise questions about whether it's a solid body or a chambered body, this is a solid body.

They simply source lightweight, high-quality materials from the market.

The slightly thinner body thickness, combined with the chambered body, creates a powerful response that repels the plucked strings—one of the model's most unique features.

The open sound, which isn't overly dark despite having 24 frets, is a major advantage of this design.

The neck is very slim, but strength is ensured by the inclusion of graphite reinforcement rods.

The pickups are JB-style in the neck position and MM-style in the bridge position.

These are original designs created specifically for Alusonic.

The pickup wiring is selected via a lever switch, which is a very unique feature.

Each position delivers a perfectly balanced sound, and each setting has a clear purpose.

Please see the bottom of the description for more details.

The preamp, equipped with a 3-band EQ, is also an original design.

Even when boosted, it doesn't become muddy; only the volume increases, and even extreme settings (such as all bands full) result in a sound that's perfectly acceptable.

This bass, these pickups, and this preamp truly feel like a perfect match; the superb synergy is a testament to their dedicated development.

While we've primarily sold Alusonic's Django DX models, this new model is the Standard.

Designed with the goal of creating a simpler model, the aluminum plate on the body top has been removed.

This is practically the only change; it's not a lower-grade model.

The result is a more open and classic nuance, both in appearance and sound.

And experiencing this model will make you realize that Alusonic's appeal isn't limited to the beauty of the aluminum plate.

Please try their latest, meticulously crafted product.

It will be delivered to you after being adjusted for optimal playability by GIB's skilled craftsmen.

○SPECS

Construction: Bolt-on
Body: Selected Sassafrass
Body Finish: Open Pore Matte Black Acrylic
Neck: Hard Maple with Graphite Reinforcing Rods
Neck Thickness: 21mm at 1st fret, 24mm at 12th fret
Neck Shape: Custom “C/V”
Neck Finish: Clear Matte Acrylic
Fretboard: Hard Maple
Fretboard Finish: Clear Matte Acrylic
Scale length: 34"" (864mm)
Radius: 16"
String Nut: PPS “Tedur”
Nut Width: 44,5 mm
Position Marks: Black Side Dots
Truss Rod: 2-way Set in Normal Position
Neck Plate: 5-Bolt
Bridge: Alusonic Aluminum with Self-Lubricating and Quick Release System
String Spacing: 18 mm
Tuners: Hipshot Ultralites Black
Pickups: Alusonic Alnico5 Humbucker / Single Coil
Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass
Hardware: Aluminum Matte Black
Pickguard: Aluminum Matte Black
Knobs: Aluminum Black
Strings: Steel 45/130
○Lever Switch
Position 1 - Humbucker Bridge
Position 2 - Humbucker Bridge + Neck
Position 3 - Neck
Position 4 - Single Coil Bridge + Neck
Position 5 - Single Coil Bridge

Accessories: Original gig bag, warranty card, instruction manual

Text: Shunsuke Saga

Inspector: Hayakawa

Product photos: Muragishi

Manufacturer's warranty included

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: 【new】Stem / Classic Style J4 Black Heavy Aged 4.1kg #0222【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,162
item.description:
$3,162 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

Stem is a brand produced by Sago New Material Guitars, a handmade brand from the Kansai region of Japan.

Stem is positioned between Sago, which specializes in custom-order production, and Seed, which achieves high quality through excellent quality control while keeping costs down by manufacturing overseas.

Stem is known for producing models that retain Sago's high quality while keeping costs down by fixing specifications and shapes.

This model is the Stem version of Sago's flagship model, the Classic Style J4.

The body is made of alder, the fingerboard is rosewood, and the neck is hard maple, a classic wood combination.

The finish is Sago's signature thin lacquer.

Furthermore, it has been given a realistic aged finish, giving it an outstanding look.

The staff who applied this aging treatment were formerly Fender craftsmen, part of the team of the highly acclaimed Master Builder Carlos Lopez.

At Carlos's team, they primarily worked on relicing, and were highly regarded both inside and outside the company.

The pickups are original L(x) BJ4s.

The winding count has been slightly increased, resulting in a pickup that blends well into an ensemble and has excellent projection.

The tuning keys are Gotoh GB528, and the bridge is a spiral saddle type.

These hardware components have also been aged, and combined with the "Heavy Aged" finish on the body, they exude the look and feel of a vintage guitar that has been played for many years.

The sound has the open nuances characteristic of the Stem.

While featuring a strong, punchy low-mid range, it has a very well-balanced output from low to high frequencies, resulting in a straightforward and easy-to-use sound.

The Stem is not simply a cheaper version of Sago.

With its exceptional paint quality, honed through training at Fender Custom Shop, and its flexibility in focusing on highly demanded custom orders, this guitar holds its own against numerous high-end instruments.

It's a truly serious brand offering value far exceeding its price.

Please give it a try.

item.title: Ashdown Roasted Saint Bass Guitar - Black Sparkle — $375
item.description:
$375 · Excellent · Clingan Guitar Tone

Ashdown Engineering is a British brand founded in 1997 by Mark Gooday — former production director at Trace Elliot — with the mission of building gear that gave bass players uncompromising tone and real character. While Ashdown built their reputation on amplifiers, their instruments carry that same philosophy: quality construction, distinctive personality, and genuine playability at every level.

The Roasted Saint is a bold, characterful bass built around a roasted maple neck and fingerboard that delivers exceptional stability and a warm, resonant feel that's immediately addictive. The P/J Alnico pickup configuration gives you a remarkable tonal palette — from thick, punchy P-bass thump to bright, articulate J-bass clarity — and the matching headstock and Black Sparkle finish give it a look as striking as its sound. This is a bass that punches well above its weight. In excellent condition and ready to gig.

All instruments sold by Clingan Guitar Tone are inspected, serviced, and set up by our professionally trained guitar and amp technicians, ensuring they perform at their absolute best. Our team's expertise and attention to detail mean you can buy with complete confidence, knowing your gear has been given the highest level of care.

Any questions, don't hesitate to ask! Located at Clingan Guitar Tone, 1/155 Donald Street, Brunswick East Melbourne. Available for viewing and playing anytime. We ship all around the world, so feel free to ask for an accurate shipping quote.

The 35” scale provides an exceptionally tight and defined low B string, while the comfortable neck and low action make this bass a joy to play. Whether you’re playing rock, funk, gospel, jazz, country, or session work, the 55-02 covers an incredible range of tones with ease.

item.title: 【new】Alusonic / Django Deluxe 4 JH GTS Stealth 3.090kg #0126549【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $6,524
item.description:
$6,524 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Alusonic is an Italian brand that primarily manufactures guitars and basses incorporating aluminum as a material.

Because they design and manufacture many elements in-house, their products are all pure, highly consistent, and possess a perfect balance at a high level.

The Django is their flagship model, featuring a refined body shape and unique electronics.

This is a new GTS Model that makes extensive use of carbon fiber.

It has finally arrived at GIB.

The body is made of Roasted Sassafras.

It is similar to Ash, but is characterized by being lighter.

It is also famous as the wood that Fender initially considered using, and in recent years, several guitar brands have started using it.

Roasted wood is also called Thermowood.

Thermowood, which was commercialized in the musical instrument field by Sago of Japan, was originally used in Finland as a building material for saunas.

Thermowood, valued for its resistance to high temperatures and humidity, is also clearly useful for musical instruments, as evidenced by its adoption by top manufacturers like Sadowsky and Suhr.

It seems that a certain amount of Thermowood is discarded during processing.

Presumably, pieces that would warp in the future are also discarded here.

Sound-wise, it has a strong, well-developed nuance.

The finish is a thin acrylic that allows the wood grain to show through.

A particularly noteworthy feature of this model is the carbon top wood.

It's positioned as an inlay on the body top and headstock, creating an elegant look and a light, crisp sound with its unique pattern and beautiful finish.

The slightly thinner body thickness and chambered body combine to create a powerful response that repels the plucked strings—one of the most unique features of this model.

The fingerboard uses carbon fiber, a new material adopted by the brand.

This is the most distinctive feature of this model.

Their expertise in creating superb basses using unconventional fretboard materials like Graphitex is evident once again.

The extremely lightweight and rigid carbon fiber provides a tight yet open playing feel, with a pleasingly responsive feel as if the strings are bouncing on the fretboard.

The neck is very slim, but strength is ensured by the inclusion of graphite reinforcement rods.

The open sound, which doesn't become too dark despite having 24 frets, is a major advantage of this design.

The pickups are PB-style in the neck position and MM-style in the bridge position.

These are original designs created specifically for the Alusonic.

The pickup wiring is selected via a lever switch, which is a very unique feature.

Each position delivers a perfectly balanced sound, and each configuration has a clear purpose.

Please see the bottom of the description for more details.

The preamp, equipped with a 2-band EQ, is also an original design. Even with a boost, the sound doesn't become muddy; only the volume increases, and even with extreme settings (such as all settings at full), it produces a pleasing sound.

It truly feels like this bass, these pickups, and this preamp are perfectly matched. The superb compatibility is a testament to their dedicated development.

While we've sold many Alusonic Django DX and Django Standard models in the past, this model showcases Alusonic's originality and meticulously calculated design balance.

As a result, the sound feels even more refined.

However, it never becomes an outlandish sound; it retains the "bass-like" tone we envision.

Please try their latest and greatest product.

○SPECS

item.title: 【used】Bacchus / WJB5-1DX-AC RSM/P PWH-MH 4.475kg 【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $638
item.description:
$638 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Bacchus is a Japanese brand based in Nagano.

It's a brand under the Deviser umbrella, which owns a large guitar factory called the Asuka Factory. The high quality, stemming from the factory's in-house production, is highly regarded by many musicians.

Bacchus offers several series at different price points, but the Universe series, in particular, pursues high cost performance.

This WJB-1DX is a JB model that prioritizes playability and sound while keeping the price down.

The body is made of poplar.

The neck is made of roasted maple, and the fingerboard is made of black-dyed purpleheart.

Roasted maple is originally a Finnish technique, and Sago, a Kansai-based brand, is considered a pioneer in its use in musical instruments.

This material is said to have increased rigidity by evaporating moisture through high-temperature processing.

Naturally, wood that warps during this process is not used.

In two senses, this results in a high-quality finish.

The pickups and preamp are original. The tone is well-developed, with a wide range and warm nuances, resulting in a juicy and joyful sound.

This is a recommended instrument for both first-time 5-string bass players and as a secondary instrument.

Fret life is approximately 85%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and finish are generally clean.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Treble, Bass
Nut Width: 45.3mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: China

During setup, we used our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

They are delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

item.title: 【new】Meta Guitars / Veil B4 Medium Scale Champagne Beige 3.135kg【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $6,898
item.description:
$6,898 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Meta Guitars is a high-end brand of modern guitars based in France.

Builder Fred has been interested in guitars and basses since childhood, and by the age of 15, he had already begun dedicating his life to stringed instrument making.

The word "meta" has meanings such as "after," "beyond," and "change."

With this concept in mind, he has worked to create beautiful, passionate, individual, modern, and revolutionary guitars and basses.

Most Meta Guitars instruments are designed without heads.

This allows for lighter weight, a more compact design, and superior ergonomic balance.

Furthermore, headless hardware does not use gears for tuning.

This means increased tuning stability.

He is committed to achieving a superior balance in both sound and playability.

To achieve this, almost all models feature a single cutaway and ergonomic curves.

The beautiful body curves not only look good, but also provide the player with ideal balance whether standing or sitting.

By using a single-cutaway neck, the neck's strength on the lower end increases, its elasticity decreases, resulting in a tighter tone and longer sustain.
As a result, a clearer, more direct tone is achieved, especially on the lower strings.

He also incorporated fanned frets to improve the sound balance.

This not only balances the tone but also provides users with a wider range of string gauge options.

Some may worry about playability, but in reality, this design is very natural in relation to the movement of the human arm and wrist.

It shouldn't take long to feel comfortable playing it.

This model is the flagship Veil model.

Within the Veil series, this model aims for a particularly rich low-to-mid range.

The scale length is 35-33 inches.

If you're looking for a more traditional bass sound, this is the one for you.

The body is made of quarter-sawn chestnut, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is ebony.

Despite weighing nearly 3kg, the tone is remarkably full, without any lack of clarity, looseness, or lightness.

It features Lace's Alua Bassbar pickups.

These extremely lightweight pickups are characterized by their transparency and speed.

The natural nuances, free from strong coloration, leave a positive impression.

The low noise level is also a major plus.

Wiring is done from below the knobs, so there is no back panel.

Overall, it's a refined and beautiful product.

Perhaps it's the foundation of European culture that allows such a product to emerge in the modern era.

Please give it a try.

●SPECS

4 strings, midscale
5A flame maple
Exotic ebony fingerboard
Top-mount electronic pickups
Ebony pots with matching fingerboard
Chestnut body
Open pore satin finish, Champagne Beige
Black Hardware
Lace Alumitone

Accessories: Gig bag, wrench
